items.title: TV presenter Dermot Murnaghan dies aged 68
items.standfirst: The former BBC, ITV and Sky News journalist has died after revealing he had stage four prostate cancer.
items.content: The former BBC, ITV and Sky News journalist has died after revealing he had stage four prostate cancer.

items.title: TV presenter Dermot Murnaghan dies after being ill with prostate cancer
items.standfirst: Murnaghan ‘died peacefully’ at his home in London on Saturday, says his familyThe TV presenter Dermot Murnaghan “died peacefully” at his home in north London on Saturday morning “following a period of illness with prostate cancer”, his family said in a statement.More details to follow … Continue reading...

clusters.title: Spain battles to contain one of its deadliest wildfires as at least 12 killed
clusters.neutral_headline: Spain battles to contain one of its deadliest wildfires as at least 12 killed
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Spain battles to contain one of its deadliest wildfires as at least 12 killed
items.standfirst: At least four Britons are believed to be among the victims of the blaze, which left another 23 people missing.
items.content: At least four Britons are believed to be among the victims of the blaze, which left another 23 people missing.

items.title: Fast-spreading wildfire kills at least 12 in southern Spain
items.standfirst: Twenty-three people missing and four Britons thought to be among those who died trying to flee Almería blaze‘I had an incredible escape’: British woman tells of close encounter with wildfireAt least 12 people have been killed and 23 are unaccounted for after one of Spain’s deadliest wildfires broke out in the south-eastern province of Almería as the country endures its second heatwave of the summer.The regional government of Andalucía said the victims, four of whom are believed to be British, had died while trying to escape the flames near the village of Bédar in the municipality of Los Gallardos. Continue reading...
items.content: Twenty-three people missing and four Britons thought to be among those who died trying to flee Almería blaze‘I had an incredible escape’: British woman tells of close encounter with wildfireAt least 12 people have been killed and 23 are unaccounted for after one of Spain’s deadliest wildfires broke out in the south-eastern province of Almería as the country endures its second heatwave of the summer.The regional government of Andalucía said the victims, four of whom are believed to be British, had died while trying to escape the flames near the village of Bédar in the municipality of Los Gallardos. items.content: Australian police have released bodycam footage showing one of the last attempts to get Bradley Murdoch to reveal the location of Peter Falconio's body, on the 25th anniversary of his murder.

clusters.title: World Cup 2022: Spain edge Belgium, set up semi-final showdown with France
clusters.neutral_headline: World Cup 2022: Spain edge Belgium, set up semi-final showdown with France
bullet_summary: Fabian Ruiz had opened the scoring before Charles De Ketelaere restored parity during the first half, but Luis de la Fuente's side found the decisive goal just two minutes from time

items.title: World Cup 2022: Spain edge Belgium, set up semi-final showdown with France
items.standfirst: Spain booked their place in the World Cup semi-finals on Friday with a dramatic 2-1 victory over Belgium, sealed by a late winner from Mikel Merino. La Roja will now face France on Tuesday for a place in the final.
items.content: Spain booked their place in the World Cup semi-finals on Friday with a dramatic 2-1 victory over Belgium, sealed by a late winner from Mikel Merino. La Roja will now face France on Tuesday for a place in the final.

items.title: Spain edges Belgium and will face France in World Cup semifinal
items.standfirst: Spain and France will meet Tuesday in Arlington, Texas, in a matchup anticipated for years. Neither team has lost at this year's World Cup.
items.content: Spain and France will meet Tuesday in Arlington, Texas, in a matchup anticipated for years. Neither team has lost at this year's World Cup.(Image credit: Mark J. Terrill)

items.title: World Cup 2026: Merino's late winner sends Spain past Belgium and into semi-finals
items.standfirst: Spain booked their place in the World Cup semi-finals on Friday after Mikel Merino struck a dramatic late winner at the 88th minute to secure a 2-1 victory over Belgium. Fabian Ruiz had opened the scoring before Charles De Ketelaere restored parity during the first half, but Luis de la Fuente's side found the decisive goal just two minutes from time.
items.content: Spain booked their place in the World Cup semi-finals on Friday after Mikel Merino struck a dramatic late winner at the 88th minute to secure a 2-1 victory over Belgium. Fabian Ruiz had opened the scoring before Charles De Ketelaere restored parity during the first half, but Luis de la Fuente's side found the decisive goal just two minutes from time.

clusters.title: Death toll in Venezuela quakes surpasses 4,000
clusters.neutral_headline: Venezuela quake death toll passes 4,000 as scale of recovery effort looms large

items.title: Death toll in Venezuela quakes surpasses 4,000
items.standfirst: The death toll from Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes has risen above 4,000, authorities said Friday, as families continued searching through the rubble for missing loved ones more than two weeks after the country's deadliest quake in over a century.
items.content: The death toll from Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes has risen above 4,000, authorities said Friday, as families continued searching through the rubble for missing loved ones more than two weeks after the country's deadliest quake in over a century.

items.title: Venezuela quake death toll passes 4,000 as scale of recovery effort looms large
items.standfirst: Nearly 17,000 injured and thousands more are listed as missing amid calls by UN and president Delcy Rodríguez for financial helpThe death toll in Venezuela’s devastating twin earthquakes has topped 4,000, the government said on Friday.At least 4,118 people were killed and 16,740 injured in the back-to-back quakes on 24 June that flattened entire districts in the coastal state of La Guaira, Venezuelan parliament chief Jorge Rodriguez wrote on Telegram. Thousands more are listed as missing. Continue reading...
items.content: Nearly 17,000 injured and thousands more are listed as missing amid calls by UN and president Delcy Rodríguez for financial helpThe death toll in Venezuela’s devastating twin earthquakes has topped 4,000, the government said on Friday.At least 4,118 people were killed and 16,740 injured in the back-to-back quakes on 24 June that flattened entire districts in the coastal state of La Guaira, Venezuelan parliament chief Jorge Rodriguez wrote on Telegram. Thousands more are listed as missing. Continue reading...

items.content: Victor Wembanyama committed his long-term future to the San Antonio Spurs on Friday, signing a reported five-year, $252 million rookie maximum contract extension after telling fans: "I'm here to stay. Whatever it takes."

clusters.title: Bipartisan housing bill becomes law after Trump refuses to sign it
clusters.neutral_headline: Bipartisan housing bill becomes law after Trump refuses to sign it
bullet_summary: Bruce Marks, founder and CEO of the Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss
Reported by CBS News, NPR News

items.title: Bipartisan housing bill becomes law after Trump refuses to sign it
items.standfirst: A landmark housing bill automatically became law overnight after President Trump declined to sign it.
items.content: A landmark housing bill automatically became law overnight after President Trump declined to sign it.

items.title: How Congress' housing bill may, or may not, impact Americans
items.standfirst: A sweeping bipartisan housing affordability bill is set to go into law at midnight on Friday, without President Trump's signature. The president says his decision not to sign the bill is a protest against lawmakers' failure to pass his package of voting reforms and restrictions. Bruce Marks, founder and CEO of the Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.
items.content: A sweeping bipartisan housing affordability bill is set to go into law at midnight on Friday, without President Trump's signature. The president says his decision not to sign the bill is a protest against lawmakers' failure to pass his package of voting reforms and restrictions. Bruce Marks, founder and CEO of the Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

items.title: Landmark bipartisan housing bill to become law without Trump's signature
items.standfirst: The landmark bipartisan housing bill is set to become law overnight into Saturday despite President Trump's refusal to sign it. CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes reports.
items.content: The landmark bipartisan housing bill is set to become law overnight into Saturday despite President Trump's refusal to sign it. CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes reports.

items.title: Largest housing affordability bill in decades becomes law without Trump's signature
items.standfirst: President Trump refused to sign the bill without Congress first passing his sweeping voter ID bill.
items.content: President Trump refused to sign the bill without Congress first passing his sweeping voter ID bill.(Image credit: Francisco Seco)

clusters.title: New York City bans deceptive subscription practices
clusters.neutral_headline: New York City bans deceptive subscription practices
bullet_summary: Reported by Reuters Top News, Guardian Business

items.title: New York City bans deceptive subscription practices
items.standfirst: New York City has adopted a rule aimed at stopping subscription traps and other deceptive recurring charges, with enforcement set to begin Oct. 1. The city is also pursuing a separate junk-fee rule that would require sellers to show the full price, including mandatory charges, up front. [theguardian.com]
items.content: New York City has adopted a rule aimed at stopping subscription traps and other deceptive recurring charges, with enforcement set to begin Oct. 1. The city is also pursuing a separate junk-fee rule that would require sellers to show the full price, including mandatory charges, up front. [theguardian.com]

items.title: New York City becomes first in the US to ban deceptive subscription practices
items.standfirst: Rule from Mamdani administration bans companies from trapping customers into paying recurring charges and ‘junk fees’New York City has adopted a new rule that bans companies from using deceptive subscriptions to trap customers into paying for gym memberships, streaming services and other recurring charges, the city’s consumer protection office said.The new rule, which will start on 1 October, promises hefty fines and aggressive enforcement for violators. Companies that do not provide a simple way to cancel could pay $525 per user subscription, back fees and additional fines. Continue reading...
items.content: Rule from Mamdani administration bans companies from trapping customers into paying recurring charges and ‘junk fees’New York City has adopted a new rule that bans companies from using deceptive subscriptions to trap customers into paying for gym memberships, streaming services and other recurring charges, the city’s consumer protection office said.The new rule, which will start on 1 October, promises hefty fines and aggressive enforcement for violators. Companies that do not provide a simple way to cancel could pay $525 per user subscription, back fees and additional fines. Continue reading...
